Content:
The Bride (2026) is a horror drama romance film rated R, with a runtime of 126 minutes. It is scheduled for release on March 6, 2026. The film is directed and written by Maggie Gyllenhaal, known for her work on The Lost Daughter. The cast includes Jessie Buckley, Christian Bale, Peter Sarsgaard, Annette Bening, Jake Gyllenhaal, and Penélope Cruz. It is produced by Warner Brothers.

The story centers on a lonely Frankenstein (Bale) who travels to 1930s Chicago to meet groundbreaking scientist Dr. Euphronious (Annette Bening) to create a companion. Together, they revive a murdered young woman, resulting in the birth of The Bride (Buckley). The film is a bold, iconoclastic take on a compelling story, blending life, macabre romance, and ambitious ideas. Maggie Gyllenhaal's vision presents a beautiful yet hideous creature that is both captivating and unsettling.

Title: Hoppers
Author: Unknown
Summary: "Hoppers," a 2026 animated sci-fi comedy directed by Daniel Chong, stars Jon Hamm and Bobby Moynihan. Released by Walt Disney Pictures on March 6, 2026, the film follows Mabel as she explores a new technology that allows human consciousness to inhabit robotic animals, uncovering mysteries in the animal world. The PG-rated film runs for 105 minutes.
Content:
Hoppers (2026)  
Action, Animated, Comedy, Sci-Fi  
Rated PG, 105 min.  
Release Date: March 6, 2026  
Director: Daniel Chong  
Writers: Jesse Andrews, Daniel Chong  
Cast: Jon Hamm, Bobby Moynihan, Piper Curda  
Studio: Walt Disney Pictures  
Running Time: 105 minutes  
MPA Rating: Rated PG for action/peril, some scary images, and mild language  

What is Hoppers about?  
In “Hoppers,” scientists have discovered how to “hop” human consciousness into lifelike robotic animals, allowing people to communicate with animals as animals! Using the new technology, Mabel (Curda) will uncover mysteries within the animal world that are beyond anything she could have imagined.  

Produced by Nicole Paradis Grindle, “Hoppers” also features the voices of Bobby Moynihan and Jon Hamm.
